speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Here is better than the open air. Take it

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: thankfully. I will piece out the comfort with what

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: addition I can. I will not be long from you.

speaker: KENT
text: All the power of his wits have given way to his

speaker: KENT
text: impatience. The gods reward your kindness!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Frateretto calls me and tells me Nero is an

speaker: EDGAR
text: angler in the lake of darkness. Pray, innocent, and

speaker: EDGAR
text: beware the foul fiend.

speaker: FOOL
text: Prithee, nuncle, tell me whether a madman be a

speaker: FOOL
text: gentleman or a yeoman.

speaker: LEAR
text: A king, a king!

speaker: FOOL
text: No, he’s a yeoman that has a gentleman to his

speaker: FOOL
text: son, for he’s a mad yeoman that sees his son a

speaker: FOOL
text: gentleman before him.

speaker: LEAR
text: To have a thousand with red burning spits

speaker: LEAR
text: Come hissing in upon ’em!

speaker: EDGAR
text: The foul fiend bites my back.

speaker: FOOL
text: He’s mad that trusts in the tameness of a wolf, a

speaker: FOOL
text: horse’s health, a boy’s love, or a whore’s oath.

speaker: LEAR
text: It shall be done. I will arraign them straight.

speaker: LEAR
text:
To
                 
                Edgar
                .
                 
              Come, sit thou here, most learnèd

speaker: LEAR
text: justice.

speaker: LEAR
text:
To
                 
                Fool
                .
                 
              Thou sapient sir, sit here. Now, you

speaker: LEAR
text: she-foxes—

speaker: EDGAR
text: Look where he stands and glares!—Want’st

speaker: EDGAR
text: thou eyes at trial, madam?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Come o’er the burn, Bessy, to me—

speaker: FOOL
text: Her boat hath a leak,

speaker: FOOL
text: And she must not speak

speaker: FOOL
text: Why she dares not come over to thee.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The foul fiend haunts Poor Tom in the voice of

speaker: EDGAR
text: a nightingale. Hoppedance cries in Tom’s belly for

speaker: EDGAR
text: two white herring.—Croak not, black angel. I have

speaker: EDGAR
text: no food for thee.

speaker: KENT
text: How do you, sir? Stand you not so amazed.

speaker: KENT
text: Will you lie down and rest upon the cushions?

speaker: LEAR
text: I’ll see their trial first. Bring in their evidence.

speaker: LEAR
text:
To
                 
                Edgar
                .
                 
              Thou robèd man of justice, take thy

speaker: LEAR
text: place,

speaker: LEAR
text:
To
                 
                Fool
                .
                 
              And thou, his yokefellow of equity,

speaker: LEAR
text:
Bench by his side.
                 
                To
                 
                Kent
                .
               You are o’ th’ commission;

speaker: LEAR
text: Sit you, too.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Let us deal justly.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Sleepest or wakest, thou jolly shepherd?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thy sheep be in the corn.

speaker: EDGAR
text: And for one blast of thy minikin mouth,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thy sheep shall take no harm.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Purr the cat is gray.

speaker: LEAR
text: Arraign her first; ’tis Goneril. I here take my oath

speaker: LEAR
text: before this honorable assembly, kicked the poor

speaker: LEAR
text: king her father.

speaker: FOOL
text: Come hither, mistress. Is your name Goneril?

speaker: LEAR
text: She cannot deny it.

speaker: FOOL
text: Cry you mercy, I took you for a joint stool.

speaker: LEAR
text: And here’s another whose warped looks proclaim

speaker: LEAR
text: What store her heart is made on. Stop her there!

speaker: LEAR
text: Arms, arms, sword, fire! Corruption in the place!

speaker: LEAR
text: False justicer, why hast thou let her ’scape?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Bless thy five wits!

speaker: KENT
text: O pity! Sir, where is the patience now

speaker: KENT
text: That you so oft have boasted to retain?

speaker: EDGAR
text: My tears begin to take his part so much

speaker: EDGAR
text: They mar my counterfeiting.

speaker: LEAR
text: The little dogs and all,

speaker: LEAR
text: Tray, Blanch, and Sweetheart, see, they bark at me.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Tom will throw his head at them.—Avaunt, you

speaker: EDGAR
text: curs!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Be thy mouth or black or white,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Tooth that poisons if it bite,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Mastiff, greyhound, mongrel grim,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Hound or spaniel, brach, or lym,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Bobtail tike, or trundle-tail,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Tom will make him weep and wail;

speaker: EDGAR
text: For, with throwing thus my head,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Dogs leapt the hatch, and all are fled.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Do de, de, de. Sessa! Come, march to wakes

speaker: EDGAR
text: and fairs and market towns. Poor Tom, thy horn

speaker: EDGAR
text: is dry.

speaker: LEAR
text: Then let them anatomize Regan; see what breeds

speaker: LEAR
text: about her heart. Is there any cause in nature that

speaker: LEAR
text:
make these hard hearts?
                 
                To
                 
                Edgar
                .
               You, sir, I

speaker: LEAR
text: entertain for one of my hundred; only I do not like

speaker: LEAR
text: the fashion of your garments. You will say they are

speaker: LEAR
text: Persian, but let them be changed.

speaker: KENT
text: Now, good my lord, lie here and rest awhile.

speaker: LEAR
text: Make no noise, make no noise.

speaker: LEAR
text: Draw the curtains. So, so, we’ll go to supper i’ th’

speaker: LEAR
text: morning.

speaker: FOOL
text: And I’ll go to bed at noon.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Come hither, friend. Where is the King my master?

speaker: KENT
text: Here, sir, but trouble him not; his wits are gone.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Good friend, I prithee, take him in thy arms.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I have o’erheard a plot of death upon him.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: There is a litter ready; lay him in ’t,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And drive toward Dover, friend, where thou shalt meet

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Both welcome and protection. Take up thy master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: If thou shouldst dally half an hour, his life,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With thine and all that offer to defend him,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Stand in assurèd loss. Take up, take up,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And follow me, that will to some provision

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Give thee quick conduct.

speaker: KENT
text: Oppressèd nature sleeps.

speaker: KENT
text: This rest might yet have balmed thy broken sinews,

speaker: KENT
text: Which, if convenience will not allow,

speaker: KENT
text:
Stand in hard cure.
                 
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Fool
                .
               Come, help to bear thy master.

speaker: KENT
text: Thou must not stay behind.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Come, come away.

speaker: EDGAR
text: When we our betters see bearing our woes,

speaker: EDGAR
text: We scarcely think our miseries our foes.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Who alone suffers suffers most i’ th’ mind,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Leaving free things and happy shows behind.

speaker: EDGAR
text: But then the mind much sufferance doth o’erskip

speaker: EDGAR
text: When grief hath mates and bearing fellowship.

speaker: EDGAR
text: How light and portable my pain seems now

speaker: EDGAR
text: When that which makes me bend makes the King bow!

speaker: EDGAR
text: He childed as I fathered. Tom, away.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Mark the high noises, and thyself bewray

speaker: EDGAR
text: When false opinion, whose wrong thoughts defile thee,

speaker: EDGAR
text: In thy just proof repeals and reconciles thee.

speaker: EDGAR
text: What will hap more tonight, safe ’scape the King!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Lurk, lurk.
